title: Understanding Centripetal Acceleration
text: Centripetal acceleration is the acceleration that keeps an object moving in a circular path. It is directed towards the center of the circle.

title: Formula for Centripetal Acceleration
text: The formula for centripetal acceleration is given by $$a_c=\frac{v^2}{r}$$, where v is the velocity of the satellite and $$r$$ is the radius of the orbit.

title: Finding the Velocity
text: The velocity of the satellite can be found using the formula $$v=\frac{2\pi r}{T}$$, where T is the period of the satellite's orbit.

title: Calculate the Velocity
text: Calculate the velocity of the satellite using the formula $$v=\frac{2\pi r}{T}$$. Use $$r=42250\times{10}^3$$ $$m$$ and $$T=86400$$ s.

title: Calculate Centripetal Acceleration
text: Now that you have the velocity, use the formula $$a_c=\frac{v^2}{r}$$ to find the centripetal acceleration. Use $$v=3074.6$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$ and $$r=42250\times{10}^3$$ $$m$$.
